summary: Added a named repo-deploy profile for `education_wemeshup`, fixed the governed Coolify operator path to run with repo-managed Python dependencies, and refreshed production from upstream `main` commit `4d27ded77559269d1a5e05b3e3aaf9acd198a0ea` so the public taxonomy continues to serve `1056` activities.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python -m pytest tests/test_lv3_cli.py tests/test_repo_deploy_profiles.py -q` passed with `55 passed in 2.91s`, `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate` returned `Repository data models OK`, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards` passed from this worktree.

`make deploy-repo-profile PROFILE=education-wemeshup-production DEPLOY_PROFILE_ARGS='--dry-run --wait'` expanded the catalog entry into the governed Coolify command, and `make coolify-manage ACTION=whoami` confirmed the private controller path plus usable deployment server.

`git ls-remote git@github.com:baditaflorin/education_wemeshup.git HEAD refs/heads/main` returned `4d27ded77559269d1a5e05b3e3aaf9acd198a0ea` for both `HEAD` and `refs/heads/main` immediately before the redeploy.

`make deploy-repo-profile PROFILE=education-wemeshup-production DEPLOY_PROFILE_ARGS='--wait --timeout 1800'` finished on attempt `1` with deployment `lwyww144yknn3fe524ou8pq7`, and the deployment payload showed the rebuilt `catalog-api` image tag `p10amt0lscl3upokas4mq45y_catalog-api:4d27ded77559269d1a5e05b3e3aaf9acd198a0ea`.

`curl -ksSI https://education-wemeshup.apps.lv3.org/ | head -n 1` returned `HTTP/2 200`, and `/api/v1/catalog/taxonomy` returned `catalogVersion: 2.0.0`, `activityCount: 1056`, and `categoryCount: 95` after the redeploy.

notes: The preferred repeatable operator path is now the named profile wrapper: `make deploy-repo-profile PROFILE=education-wemeshup-production DEPLOY_PROFILE_ARGS='--wait'`.

The underlying `coolify-manage` and profile wrapper now invoke their Python entrypoints through `uv run --with pyyaml python ...` so the governed path no longer depends on a pre-seeded global PyYAML install.
